entries.title: About the Course
entries.content:
Each year in elementary grades, students read biographies of influential people from church history. This course features the stories of Christians from Alveary's modern history rotation for the years (1800-1900).

This topic is included in the following course(s): Bible: Grades 4-6

entries.title: Placement & Combining Tips
entries.content: Those who wish to combine students in Grades 4-6 with younger students should choose either this course or the Church History for grades 1-3. If you have a 6th grader, they could be combined with the 7-8 Church History course.

rows.label: Church History: Grades 4-6
rows.books: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China||Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor

entries.content:
Permission to print for non-commercial use. See Alveary group use policy to use lessons
in a group context.

LINKS: Click text or scan the QR code in the top corner of the lesson plan pages to view
online resources associated with the lessons.

Responsibility for previewing all links rests with the teacher. All links were checked at the
time of publication; however, websites change frequently and may contain objectionable
content. Please report broken links by contacting us through our website.

books.title: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

blocks.text:
• Connect to previous lessons by asking students to recall where they left off. 

• If students have difficulty recalling, teachers might try showing them a picture from the reading, sharing something the teacher remembers, or prompting them with the first sentence of the last reading. This will often refresh their memory enough to allow them to recall.

• If students still cannot recall anything from the last reading, teachers might consider continuing anyway (with a shorter reading this time) or repeating the previous lesson, depending on the reading and the nature of the book.

• Read the chapter or selection aloud. When reading to pre-readers, point to the words as you read, even if it seems they aren't paying attention.

• Move at the students' pace during the lesson, rather than feeling that a particular number of pages must be completed. It is vitally important that teachers not overload students with more material than they can absorb in one sitting.

• Students who have difficulty sitting still or listening may benefit from a fidget of their choice, such as a piece of putty or clay or an afghan; flexible positioning, such as a wobble seat or inverted sitting; or sensory modulation, such as a weighted vest or blanket or adjustment of light, temperature, or sound.

• Students with regular difficulty engaging often benefit from having a picture to associate with the story WHILE listening. Feel free to view the artwork during the reading rather than after the narration.

• Have students retell the passage orally.

• Alternatively, learners may use words, pictures, Legos, dolls, etc., to process and convey ideas in the manner most natural to them.  

• Teachers may take turns to model.

• Narration is a complex skill requiring the coordination of several parts of the brain. If a student struggles to narrate in general, with a specific subject or book, or on one day but not others, then this is an opportunity to learn. Observe when they struggle with this or any other activity today. Notice facial expressions and body language; listen to what they say about why it is hard or how they feel about it. Take note of these observations for later, tell them that you see how hard they are working, and then offer whatever support they need to end on a positive note. If necessary, take a break for the day.
• Optional: Share a piece of fine art that illustrates the story.
• Give students an opportunity to share their thoughts on the lesson and any connections made.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 1
lessons.body:
Meeting Hudson Taylor
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ NOTE
This first Church History biography is scheduled for Term 1 and finishes in Term 2, week 6. Pace of reading can be adapted to fit the student, and if additional time is needed to finish the book, there will be time in Term 1's Week 11 and in the final week of assigned reading (Term 2, Week 6).

➜ INTRO
This year in Church History, we are reading about Christians from the past who lived between the years 1800-1900 AD. You will read about people who loved God, loved the Bible, and wanted others to know the good news that salvation is found in Jesus Christ. They followed God even when it was hard and they faced difficulty. As you read, think about what ideas inspired them. 

The first book we will read is "Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China." Hudson Taylor was a missionary to the people of China and wanted to live for God through a life of prayer and faith in his Savior. 

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.11-22 "Captain Morris gripped" - "become a Christian."

➜ VIEW
∞ Image Link: Picture of young Hudson Taylor
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
Hudson Taylor born May 1, 1832

What do you recall about last week's reading?

Trouble for Hudson
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.61-73 "Instead of having" - "control after all."

• This week and last week's readings have included some ways Taylor trusted God for money and financial support.  What do you think about those approaches and ideas?

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 6
lessons.body:
Remain in England? 
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.75-86 "Back in Barnsley" - "closer to Shanghai."

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 7
lessons.body:
China At Last!
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.87-98 "Hudson looked over" - "or a minister?"

• Hudson wrote about the feelings he had as he thought about landing and leaving the Dumfries: "What peculiar feelings arise at the prospect of soon landing in an unknown country, in the midst of strangers—a country now to be my home and sphere of labor..." (p.88) Can you imagine yourself in that situation? What feelings would you have? What would you be hesitant or fearful about? What would you be excited about?
lessons.teacherNotes:
• IMPORTANT DATES
1854 - Hudson Taylor arrives in Shanghai, China

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 8
lessons.body:
"If Only They Knew the Living God"
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.99-111 "This last question" - "of a war."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.110 where it discusses beheading and the beliefs the Chinese had about this method of death, as well as the following paragraphs where it discusses injuries the coolies received.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 9
lessons.body:
One of the Crowd
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
p.111-123 "Worst of all" - "in the town."

• Even though Taylor keeps facing very difficult situations, he still wants to be in China. Why? And what is it that motivates him to stay? What do you think about his decision to continue on in China? 

➜ MAP ACTIVITY
Look at the map at the beginning of the book (unmarked p.7). Locate Shanghai, and then find Yang-chow. Locate the Yangtze River. Do you recognize any other locations on the map from Hudson Taylor's story?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.118 where the aftermath of a battle scene is described, as well as p.121-122 where Hudson and his friend are attacked by a drunken man.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 10
lessons.body:
The Luckiest Man Alive
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.124-135 "The mandarin nodded" - "the tile roof."

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
∞ Image Link: Hudson Taylor as a Missionary in China
Do you think Hudson Taylor's choice to dress similar to how a Chinese man would dress at the time a good idea? Why or why not?

What do you recall about last week's reading?

p.147-158 "Hudson, Maria cuddling" - "took a collection."

⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS   
p.170-180 "For two nights" - "nearby ships thought."

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
∞ Image Link: Hudson Taylor & His Wife with a Group of Chinese Christians
What do you notice about this picture? What do you notice about the clothing and shoes the group is wearing?

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 17
lessons.body:
The Work Begins
⍞ Materials: Hudson Taylor: Deep in the Heart of China

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
∞ Image Link: Yes, We Part, But Not Forever
View the hymn titled "Yes We Part, But Not Forever." Mentioned in yesterday's reading, the missionaries sang it together as they parted ways. Read the words (optionally, if someone is able to, play the melody so you can hear the tune). How do you think this hymn comforted the hearts of the missionaries? 

➜ READ & NARRATE  
p.181-193 "Mr. Brunton traveled" - "and became fruitful."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.184 where Taylor's young daughter Grace dies. Preview the rest of the chapter as the mob chants untruths about the missionaries and threatens to kill them.

➜ READ & NARRATE
p.195-204 "By Christmas 1869" - "heart of China."
lessons.teacherNotes:
• EXTRA HELPING
∞ Video Link: History of OMF
View the short video on the history of OMF International (the current name of Hudson Taylor's China Inland Mission).

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 19
lessons.body:
The White Ma and the Royal Canoe
⍞ Materials: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor

NOTE: p.5 has a short explanation of how the authors adapted stories from Slessor's life for the book. 

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ INTRO
Our next book in this year's Church History is about Mary Slessor, a woman who left Scotland to be a missionary in Calabar, which is in West Africa.  

➜ READ & NARRATE 
p.9-18 "Pressing her body" - "the missing goat."

➜ VIEW & DISCUSS
∞ Video Link: Acts 3 in Efik language 
Imatu is surprised when Mary can speak in the Efik language. Visit the link to hear verses from Acts 3 spoken in the Efik language.
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.17-18 where mention is made of the chief being drunk.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 20
lessons.body:
"Run, Ma, Run!"
⍞ Materials: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor, wall map or globe

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ MAP WORK
∞ Map Link: Map of Nigeria
Find Nigeria in West Africa on your wall map or globe. Use your map or globe or visit the link to find Calabar, a city in what is now the country of Nigeria. 

➜ READ & NARRATE 
p.19-27 "Chief Edem was" - "Run, Ma, Run!"

• When you read about how Mary Slessor talks to the children, or to the men helping her bring the supplies, or to the chiefs of the villages, what is your reaction? Do you think she is brave? Do you think she is not making very good choices, putting herself in danger? Or is there a different way you would describe Mary?
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview passage on p.19 where mention is made of the drinking at the funeral and of the drunk chief. Preview p.20 where worship of the "angry spirits" is mentioned.

lessons.title: 20m Church History: Grades 4-6 - Lesson 21

What Would White Ma Do? 
⍞ Materials: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor

➜ RECAP
What do you recall about last week's reading?

➜ VIEW IMAGE
∞ Image Link: Mary Slessor, Missionary

➜ READ & NARRATE 
p.28-36 "Dropping her slate" - "her voice quivering."
lessons.teacherNotes:
★ TEACHER NOTE
Preview p.28 and p.30 where the belief regarding twins is discussed. This is a main point in the book, as Mary Slessor works hard to save twins from death.

⍞ Materials: Trial by Poison: Mary Slessor

➜ RECAP

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS  
p.37-45 "We lived in" - "give one less!"

• The punishments used by the chief are harsh. What do you think of Mary Slessor's actions to stand up for Imatu to reduce the punishment?

★ TEACHER NOTE
On p.63, the word "cannibal" is used to describe the warriors. On p.70-71, trading gin and drinking are referenced.
